work: Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?

work: Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?

significance: The resilient small entrepreneur — PKD's admiration for the adaptable ordinary person

wordplay_note: His name is his situation — orbiting alone in a dangerous field
brief: Astronaut stranded in orbit who becomes the world's DJ and cultural lifeline
significance: The voice from above — media as the last thread of civilization

etymology: Virgil = guide (as in Dante's guide); Ackerman = field-man
wordplay_note: A guide through the fields of simulated politics
brief: Elderly industrialist and last person who remembers the real Nicole Thibodeaux
significance: Memory as resistance — the man who knows the First Lady is a robot

wordplay_note: A brown warrior — embattled and weary
brief: Psychokinetic jug player who becomes entangled in the regime's power struggles
significance: Art and telekinetic talent as political currency

wordplay_note: A congregation of neuroses — he is deeply disturbed
brief: Psychokinetic pianist who is so neurotic he believes he is becoming invisible
significance: The artist as basket case — genius and madness inseparable

wordplay_note: Everyman name — a common man in an uncommon world
brief: Citizen who seeks passage to Mars to escape the Simulacra regime
significance: The common man trying to flee the political machine

wordplay_note: A proper English name suggesting establishment
brief: Der Alte — the secret power behind the presidential puppet
significance: The man behind the curtain — real power hiding behind simulated authority

wordplay_note: A garden devastated — paradise lost through alien games
brief: Member of Pretty Blue Fox game-playing group on a depopulated Earth
significance: Games as the last human activity when reproduction has nearly ceased

wordplay_note: Pete's wife in the Bindmen game rotation
brief: One of the rotating spouses in the Titan-mandated game
significance: Marriage as game mechanic — relationships reduced to strategic moves

wordplay_note: A cat among the game-players — predatory and sly
brief: Vugs (Titanian aliens) using the Bindmen game to colonize Earth
significance: The alien infiltrator disguised as a player

wordplay_note: Pete's murdered wife whose death drives the plot
brief: Pete Garden's wife whose murder reveals the Vugs' conspiracy
significance: Death as the clue that the game is rigged

wordplay_note: Echoes J.R. Isidore — the holy fool, the crap artist
brief: Autodidact and collector of useless facts who narrates his sister's destruction
significance: The naif whose clear-eyed observation reveals everyone else's delusions

wordplay_note: The fairy skeptic — magical and destructive
brief: Jack's manipulative sister whose affairs and schemes destroy those around her
significance: The realistic novel's version of PKD's cosmic manipulator — a domestic demiurge

wordplay_note: Fay's husband who is driven to attempted murder
brief: Fay's long-suffering husband who she drives to the breaking point
significance: The ordinary man destroyed by a force of nature in human form

wordplay_note: He gets his portion — and it destroys him
brief: Young man drawn into Fay's orbit who becomes her lover and tool
significance: The naive intellectual consumed by a stronger personality

wordplay_note: Radio station manager in 1950s San Francisco
brief: Protagonist whose midlife crisis plays out against the bohemian youth culture
significance: The adult world failing to connect with the emerging counterculture

wordplay_note: A gray figure in a gray moral landscape
brief: Young woman in Bruce's orbit
significance: Moral ambiguity in domestic fiction

etymology: Fergesson = son of Fergus (man of vigor)
wordplay_note: A vigorous man whose vigor fails him
brief: Small-town businessman whose marriage and enterprise slowly collapse
significance: The small businessman — PKD's recurring tragic hero of American commerce

wordplay_note: Willing but unable to save the marriage or business
brief: Jim's older wife and former teacher whose past catches up with them
significance: Age difference and class anxiety in 1950s small-town America

wordplay_note: The lumpy traveling salesman — ungainly and unglamorous
brief: Traveling salesman whose territory Jim enters both literally and metaphorically
significance: The territory as metaphor — every man's commercial and emotional domain

work: Puttering About in a Small Land
wordplay_note: A valley man — small-scale and enclosed
brief: Husband in a failing 1950s marriage who runs a TV repair shop
significance: The putterer — doing small things in a small land while the marriage crumbles

work: Puttering About in a Small Land
wordplay_note: Roger's wife who has an affair
brief: Roger's wife whose dissatisfaction drives the novel
significance: Suburban discontent — the realistic precursor to PKD's cosmic alienation

wordplay_note: A field voice shouting from the margins
brief: Young TV salesman in postwar Oakland struggling with existential rage
significance: The angry young man — PKD's portrait of postwar American disillusionment

wordplay_note: A western castle that crumbles
brief: Aging car mechanic in Oakland whose world collapses around him
significance: The fall — Humpty Dumpty as the fragile American working man

wordplay_note: Repairman who discovers the dimensional crack
brief: TV repairman who accidentally opens a portal to another Earth
significance: The working-class discoverer — great revelations come from ordinary labor

wordplay_note: Philip K. Dick as a character — the skeptical rational half
brief: Author and narrator who is the skeptical counterpart to Horselover Fat
significance: The author watching himself go mad — or become enlightened

wordplay_note: One of the three friends investigating VALIS
brief: Phil and Fat's friend who represents a cynical Catholic perspective
significance: Catholic cynicism as one lens on the divine

wordplay_note: The beloved — a Buddhist perspective
brief: Phil and Fat's friend who represents a Buddhist-influenced rationalism
significance: Buddhism as another interpretive framework for the VALIS experience

wordplay_note: Town of lamps — illumination
brief: Rock musician and filmmaker who channels VALIS through the movie Valis
significance: Art as vessel for divine information — the artist as unconscious prophet

wordplay_note: Eric's wife and collaborator
brief: Co-creator of the Valis film and Mother Goose figure
significance: The divine feminine appearing through pop culture

wordplay_note: Small wisdom — the divine compressed into a two-year-old
brief: Two-year-old daughter of the Lamptons who is the incarnation of divine wisdom
significance: God as infant — wisdom in the smallest and most vulnerable form

wordplay_note: Horselover Fat's friend
brief: Character in the VALIS novel (distinct from Radio Free Albemuth's Nicholas Brady)
significance: The friend who witnesses the visionary's transformation

wordplay_note: Woman whose cancer prompts Fat's theological crisis
brief: Cancer patient whose suffering and death challenge Fat's belief in a benevolent VALIS
significance: Theodicy — if God is real why does Sherri die?

wordplay_note: Fat's ex-wife
brief: Horselover Fat's former wife who left him
significance: The domestic wreckage of visionary obsession

wordplay_note: Woman whose suicide triggers Fat's breakdown
brief: Friend whose suicide is the original trauma driving Fat's search for meaning
significance: Death as the engine of theological questioning

wordplay_note: The twin — Fat's first-century alter ego who coexists with him
brief: First-century Christian apostle who Fat believes lives within him as a second personality
significance: Temporal bilocation — the ancient twin inhabiting the modern self

wordplay_note: Technician and follower
brief: Minor character in the lottery world
significance: The technician serving the random system

wordplay_note: Marching to a different drummer through teleportation
brief: Character involved with Trails of Hoffman teleportation company
significance: Teleportation as emigration — the lie of the promised land

etymology: Rachmael = mercy of God; ben Applebaum = son of the apple tree
wordplay_note: God's mercy from the apple tree — mercy from the garden
brief: Heir to a spaceship line who investigates whether the colony world Whale's Mouth is real
significance: The seeker who must physically travel to discover what teleportation conceals

etymology: Freya = Norse goddess of love; Holm = small island

brief: Rachmael's love interest and companion
significance: Romance as motivation for truth-seeking

wordplay_note: Runs the Lies Incorporated deception apparatus
brief: Head of an organization that manufactures disinformation
significance: The professional liar — an entire industry of deception

wordplay_note: Jack's wife
brief: Jack's wife who accompanies him through the subjective realities
significance: The companion through the looking-glass

wordplay_note: A man named Laws who is lawless in his subjective world
brief: One of the bevatron accident survivors
significance: The irony of names — Laws creates a lawless reality

wordplay_note: Religious fanatic whose world comes first
brief: The first person whose subjective reality engulfs the group
significance: Religious fanaticism as literal world-building

wordplay_note: A singing name for a boy on a musical adventure
brief: Boy who travels to the Plowman's Planet with his cat Horace
significance: PKD's only children's book — wonder and danger on an alien world

wordplay_note: A dignified name for a cat
brief: Peter's cat who plays a crucial role in the confrontation with Glimmung
significance: The wise animal — PKD loved cats and gave them narrative importance

wordplay_note: Common name suggesting everyman
brief: The titular Nick
significance: The ordinary name in the extraordinary situation

wordplay_note: The ruler who cannot rule the future
brief: Protagonist living under a one-world government where Jones predicts the future
significance: Free agency under determinism — how to act when the future is known

wordplay_note: The gray everyman who sees ahead — a common man with uncommon foresight
brief: Mutant precognitive who becomes a demagogue by predicting the future
significance: The prophet as fascist — foreknowledge as political weapon

wordplay_note: Simple name suggesting simplicity
brief: Floyd Jones's lover
significance: The woman caught in the precognitive's orbit

wordplay_note: The director who serves the machine
brief: Director of Unity who serves the supercomputer Vulcan 3
significance: The bureaucrat as servant of artificial intelligence

wordplay_note: The protagonist who questions the machine
brief: Functionary who begins to question Unity's reliance on Vulcan 3
